zh.Montgomery, Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mabli II ferch Philip於大約1324年出生在威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利時，她的父親Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd48歲，母親Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn43歲。她於大約1357年與Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mabli II ferch Philip于大约1324年出生在威尔士蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利时，她的父亲Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd48岁，母亲Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn43岁。她于大约1357年与Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

variants.name: McKillip
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’ (see Philip ). See also McPhillips .

variants.name: Felipe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Juana, Miguel, Orlando, Pedro, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Jesus.
Spanish: from the personal name Felipe, an equivalent of Philippe .

variants.name: Lips
meanings: North German, Flemish, and Dutch: from a shortened form of the personal name Philips, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Lipps
meanings: North German: from a shortened form of the personal name Philipps, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Pepin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Camille, Emile, Pierre, Gilles, Lucien, Normand, Alcide, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Pépin) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Pipin, of uncertain, probably onomatopoeic origin. The name was borne by several Frankish kings, most notably Pepin le Bref, father of Charlemagne, and remained popular throughout the early Middle Ages.
English: from the unrecorded Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal names Pipin, Pepin, Pipun, pet forms of Philip . Compare Pipe .

variants.name: Lippens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alfons, Alphons.  French Andre.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from a shortened form of the personal name   Philip  .

variants.name: Philbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Philbín, ‘son of Philbin (Irish Philbín or Filibín)’, a pet form of Philip .

variants.name: Lipman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mayer, Chaim, Dov, Isadore, Jakob, Meyer, Pinchas.

Dutch: habitational name from a farm named Lipman or perhaps Lippink, referring to a certain Lippo and his kin. Lippo is a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name composed with the element leiba ‘offspring, son’, or of the personal name Philip .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Lipman, derived from Middle High German words liep ‘dear, beloved’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Philipose
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Philipose, derived from Greek Philippos (see Philip ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Philibert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Fernand, Mirielle, Pascale, Pierrot.

French: from the personal name Philibert, from ancient Germanic Filuberht, composed of the elements fila ‘much, very’ + berht ‘bright’, which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, abbot of Jumièges. The spelling has been influenced by personal names of Greek origin based on philein ‘to love’, e.g. Philip .

nameMeanings.name: Mabli
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Màiri .

variants.name: Màiri
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Mary .

variants.name: Mary
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: From the vocabulary word denoting the plant (Old English fearn). Modern use of this word as a given name dates from the 19th century, when it is also recorded occasionally as a boy's name in the United States. In some instances the given name may be a transferred use of the surname derived in the medieval period from the vocabulary word.

en.lifeSummary: When Mabli II ferch Philip was born about 1324, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, her father, Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd, was 48 and her mother, Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn, was 43. She married Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn about 1357, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mabli II ferch Philip est née environ 1324, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, son père, Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn environ 1357, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mabli II ferch Philip um 1324 in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd 48 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn 43. Sie hat um 1357 in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mabli II ferch Philip è nata il circa 1324 nel Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, sua padre, Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn circa 1357, nel Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabli II ferch Philipが約1324年にウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーで生まれた時，父Philip "Fongam" ap Mareduddは48歳，母Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwynは43歳であった。約1357年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーでIeuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabli II ferch Philip(이)가 대략 1324년 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 대략 1357년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mabli II ferch Philip nasceu aproximadamente 1324, em Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, seu pai, Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn aproximadamente 1357, em Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mabli II ferch Philip родилась около 1324 г. в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn около 1357 г. в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mabli II ferch Philip nació aproximadamente 1324, en Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, su papá, Philip "Fongam" ap Maredudd, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Mrs. Philip Fongam ap Maredudd Benwyn, tenía 43 años. Se casó con Ieuan Llwyd ap Llywelyn of Mathavarn aproximadamente 1357, en Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija.
